privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the DFTT #8 JPEG Search test disk image created by Brian Carrier, located at `/home/ga/evidence/jpeg_search.dd`. I verified via `fsstat` that the image is actually an NTFS volume, explicitly contradicting the task description's claim that it is a 'FAT32 camera SD card image'. This contradiction triggers a critical defect in `setup_task.sh`: the ground-truth generation script uses `istat` and strictly parses for FAT/EXT keywords ('Sectors:', 'Blocks:', 'Cluster Runs:'), ignoring the NTFS-specific '$DATA' attribute block listings. Consequently, the ground-truth script perpetually fails to map any allocated blocks, mathematically assigning a `run_count` of exactly 0 to all 15 files, setting the target second run sector to `-1`, and the expected hash to `"N/A"`. Because the `verifier.py` logic explicitly requires `gt_sector != -1` and `gt_hash != "N/A"` to award the final 30 points for Split Point ID and Raw Sector Extraction, it is mathematically impossible for any agent to achieve a score higher than 70/100. Moreover, to even pass at the 70-point threshold, an agent would have to mimic the bug by incorrectly concluding that all files have zero fragments, despite `inode` 44 empirically having 6 runs.
